theme: fewest LEGO types that build themselves
title: Self-Assembling Chimney: Fewest Brick Types
strategies: one-tile-per-cell map: a boustrophedon path visits every cell of the k x (T+1) target once, each step a brand-new single-use glue -- always correct, cost = area

row-unary counter: reuse one tile type across an entire row (rows look identical left-to-right) but still mint one fresh type per row index to know when to stop -- cost linear in T, ignores any lateral/column interaction

randomized local hill-climb over small hand-tuned tile sets, checked by re-running the simulator and keeping whatever doesn't blow up -- can shave a few types off the unary recipe but not the underlying O(T) scaling

temperature-2 cooperative binary/ripple-borrow counter: seed row 0 with T's own bits (k tiles), then reuse a small O(1)-per-column full-adder-style rule at every later row; the most-significant column's missing 'still borrowing after reading zero' tile is exactly the halt condition -- cost O(log T)
